tests.name: Gene to Protein
tests.why: Researchers need protein sequences from gene symbols for structure prediction and functional studies

tests.name: Gene to Interactions
tests.why: Network biology - find protein interaction partners to understand function and pathways

tests.name: Gene to Pathways
tests.why: Systems biology - identify biological pathways for disease mechanism studies

tests.name: Protein to Gene
tests.why: Proteomics - map protein IDs back to genes for functional interpretation

tests.name: Gene to GO Terms
tests.why: Functional annotation - understand molecular function and biological process

tests.name: Gene to Structure
tests.why: Structural biology - get 3D protein structures for drug design and function studies

tests.name: Disease to Genes
tests.why: Translational medicine - find candidate genes for disease studies and therapeutic targets

tests.name: Compound to Targets
tests.why: Drug discovery - understand which proteins a compound binds for mechanism of action

tests.name: RNA to Protein
tests.why: Transcriptomics - link RNA-seq data to protein information for multi-omics analysis

tests.name: Gene to Taxonomy
tests.why: Comparative genomics - ensure correct organism context
